_provenance.anchor: internal-contrast-only (NO human-comparison claim; author-stipulated balanced homonyms)
_provenance.instrument: inherited verbatim from cross-level lexical leg (SAME/DIFFERENT/UNCLEAR + 0-100 conf)
_provenance.note: Balanced (bal) contexts leave the homonym's two unrelated senses both licensed with no tie-break; sense-fixed contexts (a1/a2/b1) fix one sense. The disjunctive item pairs a balanced ctx1 with a sense-A-fixed ctx2 so the same/different-SENSE judgment is genuinely both/neither. NO probe was run when this file was authored.

items.senseA: the sloping edge of a river
items.senseB: a financial institution
items.ctx1: Early the next morning, once the heavy rain had finally stopped, the two of us walked all the way down to the bank and stood there together quietly for a while.
items.ctx2: Laughing and shouting to each other, the boys slid down the steep grassy bank and waded straight out into the cold shallow water of the slow river.

items.ctx2: Moving with great care, the crane waded slowly through the shallow reed-fringed marsh, lowered its long grey neck, and in one swift motion speared a small silver fish.
